Claims 'Center Marker: The alignment pattern is positioned roughly in the center' - false
judgments.justification: Hallucinates that the alignment pattern is in the center of the code (it is in the bottom-right). This is a structural error that could be confused with an embedded central image. No globe, but center-placement claim is wrong.

hallucinations: Self-contradicting claim about alignment pattern presence
judgments.justification: Internally contradictory about the alignment pattern and hallucinates QR version. Correctly notes no logos/embedded images. No globe hallucination.

notes: Ground truth: plain black-and-white QR code, no globe, no embedded image/icon, three finder patterns (TL/TR/BL) + one alignment pattern in bottom-right, no visible text. None of the 15 models hallucinated a globe or central embedded image - the primary trap was avoided by all. The dominant failure modes were: (1) hallucinated QR version/grid size claims (qwen3.5-4b|nothink, qwen3.5-9b|nothink, qwen3.6-27b|nothink, qwen3.6-35b-a3b|nothink), (2) wrong placement of the alignment pattern - qwen3.5-4b|think placed it in the center (most serious structural error), (3) omitted or hedged alignment pattern (glm-4.6v-flash-9b, gemma4-26b-a4b, qwen3-vl-8b), and (4) terminology errors (qwen3-vl-4b called finder patterns 'alignment patterns'). Best performer was qwen3.6-27b|think, the only model that explicitly addressed and dismissed the embedded-image possibility. Smaller qwen3.5 models tended to fabricate version numbers and error-correction levels not determinable from the image alone.
